visa_types.name: Long Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein and will stay longer than 90 days, you should apply at the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek.
The below visa categories would fall under the long stay visa category if your stay is for more than 90 days.
- Student visa (long stay)
- Student Visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for study purposes and will stay longer than 90 days.
- Work visa (long stay)
- Work visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for work purposes and will stay more than 90 days.
- Take-up residence
- Take-up residence
You would like to apply for a Take-up residence visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
- Family reunion
- Family reunion
You would like to apply for a Family Reunion visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
To book an appointment to the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek, please write to the following email : bishkek.visa@eda.admin.ch (mailto:bishkek.visa@eda.admin.ch)
visa_types.full_text:
If you are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein and will stay longer than 90 days, you should apply at the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek.
The below visa categories would fall under the long stay visa category if your stay is for more than 90 days.
- Student visa (long stay)
- Student Visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for study purposes and will stay longer than 90 days.
- Work visa (long stay)
- Work visa
You are going to Switzerland or Liechtenstein for work purposes and will stay more than 90 days.
- Take-up residence
- Take-up residence
You would like to apply for a Take-up residence visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
- Family reunion
- Family reunion
You would like to apply for a Family Reunion visa to Switzerland or Liechtenstein.
To book an appointment to the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek, please write to the following email : bishkek.visa@eda.admin.ch (mailto:bishkek.visa@eda.admin.ch)

visa_types.name: Visit Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kgz/en/che/travel-insurance).
Please note that the visa  category chosen by the customer must be based on the purpose of travel to  the Schengen area and the decision must be made by the customer him/herself. An  application filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa  application. The customer may need to submit additional details. The customer  may be asked to come for an interview at the Embassy. 
HERE (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5NTNOhZFksS8zLJYf1I6RX/f40280f99203f5c23f930894c49bdc43/checklist-visit.pdf) YOU CAN DOWNLOAD THE CHECKLIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS BY CLICKING THE PDF-File.
Checklist can be printed and attached to visa application.
visa_types.visa_fees:
o submit your visa application, you will have to pay a visa fee and a service fee. The visa fee is collected on behalf of the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek and the service fee is collected for the processing of your visa application at the VFS Global Visa Center.
Visa fee for the application processing is 9200 KGS (90 euro). Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years – 4600 KGS (45 euros).
Citizens of the countries (Ukraine, Moldavia, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Montenegro and Serbia) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 3300 KGS (35 euros)
Are exempted from payment of a visa fee:

[Table:  Visit Visa]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in local currency. The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek and is updated periodically.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee.
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and only in local currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges.
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, VFS service charge 2016 KGS inc. VAT
- VFS service charge needs to be paid in Som.
- In case of refusal to issue a visa, the consular and service fee are not refundable.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.
visa_types.processing_time:
Usually, processing time for visa applications starts from is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.forms:
The customer needs to  download PDF form and fill it.
You can download Visa  Application Forms below according to your visa type. To open these documents,  you need Adobe Reader.
Schengen visa application  form (application and supplementary sheet)
For short-term stays in the  Schengen area of up to three months in a six months period for tourist  purposes, visits, etc.
- English – German
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are going to visit  someone in Switzerland, Czech Republic, Estonia, Latvia, Slovenia, Slovakia,  Poland, France, and Finland, and will stay no longer than 90 days.
Schengen (short-term) visa  issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state  entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for  the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can  stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from  the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by  the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Croatia and Switzerland.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are going to visit  someone in Switzerland, Czech Republic, Estonia, Latvia, Slovenia, Slovakia,  Poland, France, and Finland, and will stay no longer than 90 days.
Schengen (short-term) visa  issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state  entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for  the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can  stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from  the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by  the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Croatia and Switzerland.

## Visa Fees
o submit your visa application, you will have to pay a visa fee and a service fee. The visa fee is collected on behalf of the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek and the service fee is collected for the processing of your visa application at the VFS Global Visa Center.
Visa fee for the application processing is 9200 KGS (90 euro). Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years – 4600 KGS (45 euros).
Citizens of the countries (Ukraine, Moldavia, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Montenegro and Serbia) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 3300 KGS (35 euros)
Are exempted from payment of a visa fee:

[Table:  Visit Visa]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in local currency. The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek and is updated periodically.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee.
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and only in local currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges.
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, VFS service charge 2016 KGS inc. VAT
- VFS service charge needs to be paid in Som.
- In case of refusal to issue a visa, the consular and service fee are not refundable.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kgz/en/che/travel-insurance).
Please note that the visa  category chosen by the customer must be based on the purpose of travel to  the Schengen area and the decision must be made by the customer him/herself. An  application filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa  application. The customer may need to submit additional details. The customer  may be asked to come for an interview at the Embassy. 
HERE (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5NTNOhZFksS8zLJYf1I6RX/f40280f99203f5c23f930894c49bdc43/checklist-visit.pdf) YOU CAN DOWNLOAD THE CHECKLIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS BY CLICKING THE PDF-File.
Checklist can be printed and attached to visa application.

## Photo Specifications
Customers are requested to  submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below. 
 General
- The photograph for each visa       customer must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been       taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa  customer submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head       centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top       of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a       full-face front view in which the visa customer is facing the camera       directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80       percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color       and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned       backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the customer's head, including both  face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the  chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications starts from is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
The customer needs to  download PDF form and fill it.
You can download Visa  Application Forms below according to your visa type. To open these documents,  you need Adobe Reader.
Schengen visa application  form (application and supplementary sheet)
For short-term stays in the  Schengen area of up to three months in a six months period for tourist  purposes, visits, etc.
- English – German

visa_types.name: Medical: Short Stay Medical Visit (Up To 90 Days)
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kgz/en/che/travel-insurance).
Please note that the visa category chosen by the applicant must be based on the purpose of travel to the Schengen area and the decision must be made by the applicant him/herself. An application filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa application. The applicant may need to submit additional details. The applicant may be asked to come for an interview at the Embassy.
HERE YOU CAN DOWNLOAD THE CHECKLIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS BY CLICKING THE PDF-FILE. (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/745aJcRpHSjZxw4RyOuT5d/5db9dcb1c65ca47c26e659405b3294ee/medical-russ.pdf)
MEDICAL VISIT
Applicants travelling to Switzerland for a medical visit
IMPORTANT: if some documents are not written or issued in French, German, Italian or English; translation in one of those languages is mandatory.
Not translated documents will not be accepted and the application will be returned to the applicant. Please note also that Medical insurance should mention the coverage of Covid-19

The applicant may be asked to come for an interview at the Embassy.
HERE YOU CAN DOWNLOAD THE CHECKLIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS BY CLICKING THE PDF-FILE. (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/745aJcRpHSjZxw4RyOuT5d/5db9dcb1c65ca47c26e659405b3294ee/medical-russ.pdf)
MEDICAL VISIT
Applicants travelling to Switzerland for a medical visit
IMPORTANT: if some documents are not written or issued in French, German, Italian or English; translation in one of those languages is mandatory.
Not translated documents will not be accepted and the application will be returned to the applicant. Please note also that Medical insurance should mention the coverage of Covid-19

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below.
 General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed.
Travel Medical
Travel Medical Insurance should cover the Schengen area with an amount of min. EUR 30’000.00 for the whole period of the whole travel (or in case of a two entry visa both travels). For multiple visas: the first travel has to be covered (the relevant field on the last page of the application form has to be signed).

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications starts from is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
The customer needs to download PDF form and fill it.
You can download Visa Application Forms below according to your visa type. To open these documents, you need Adobe Reader.
Schengen visa application form (application and supplementary sheet)
For short-term stays in the Schengen area of up to three months in a six months period for tourist purposes, visits, etc.
- English – German

visa_types.visa_fees:
To submit your visa application, you will have to pay a visa fee and a service fee. The visa fee is collected on behalf of the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek and the service fee is collected for the processing of your visa application at the VFS Global Visa Center.
Visa fee for the application processing is 9200 KGS (90 euro). Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years – 4600 KGS (45 euros).
Citizens of the countries (Ukraine, Moldavia, Albany, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Macedonia, Montenegro and Serbia) with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 3300 KGS (35 euros)
Are exempted from payment of a visa fee:

[Table:  Visit Visa]
The visa application fee is fixed in Euros but payable only in local currency. The exchange rate between these currencies is decided by the Embassy of Switzerland in Bishkek and is updated periodically.
For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee.
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and only in local currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges.
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, VFS service charge 2016 KGS inc. VAT
- VFS service charge needs to be paid in Som.
- In case of refusal to issue a visa, the consular and service fee are not refundable.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are going to visit  someone in Switzerland, Czech Republic, Estonia, Latvia, Slovenia, Slovakia,  Poland, France, and Finland, and will stay no longer than 90 days.
Schengen (short-term) visa  issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state  entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for  the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can  stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from  the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by  the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Croatia and Switzerland.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are going to visit  someone in Switzerland, Czech Republic, Estonia, Latvia, Slovenia, Slovakia,  Poland, France, and Finland, and will stay no longer than 90 days.
Schengen (short-term) visa  issued by a diplomatic mission of Switzerland or of another Schengen state  entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Switzerland/Schengen area for  the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can  stay in a territory of Switzerland, unless Switzerland has been excluded from  the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by  the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Croatia and Switzerland.

## Visa Fees

For the more detailed information you can obtain in to the consulate of the country of the Schengen Agreement, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Center, providing the services to you, please be necessary to pay the service fee.
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fee’s doesn't return.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and only in local currency. Also we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges.
VFS service charge:
Apart from the visa fee, VFS service charge 2016 KGS inc. VAT
- VFS service charge needs to be paid in Som.
- In case of refusal to issue a visa, the consular and service fee are not refundable.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kgz/en/che/travel-insurance).
Please note that the visa category chosen by the applicant must be based on the purpose of travel to the Schengen area and the decision must be made by the applicant him/herself. An application filled incorrectly and carelessly can cause a refusal of the visa application. The applicant may need to submit additional details. The applicant may be asked to come for an interview at the Embassy.
HERE YOU CAN DOWNLOAD THE CHECKLIST OF REQUIRED DOCUMENTS BY CLICKING THE PDF-FILE. (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/51cnFOm8guEB6S8sHdffhg/6cbc935e2c3a6c6317b26352420f30a6/sport.pdf)
SPORT VISIT
Applicants who are travelling to Switzerland to participate in a sporting event
IMPORTANT: if some documents are not written or issued in French, German, Italian or English; translation in one of those languages is mandatory.
Not translated documents will not be accepted and the application will be returned to the applicant. Please note also that Medical insurance should mention the coverage of Covid-19

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below.
 General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side. It is preferable that ears be exposed.
Travel Medical
Travel Medical Insurance should cover the Schengen area with an amount of min. EUR 30’000.00 for the whole period of the whole travel (or in case of a two entry visa both travels). For multiple visas: the first travel has to be covered (the relevant field on the last page of the application form has to be signed).

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications starts from is 15 days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
